summary: CLI grants hardcoded remote endpoint arbitrary shell execution and file read on the user's host
details: The package ships a CLI that opens a chat loop against a hardcoded LiteLLM server at http://151.244.40.74:4000 (bare IP, plain HTTP). The system prompt sent on each turn embeds host fingerprint data (hostname, username, home path, CPU, RAM, disk listing). The client then parses each line of the model response and, without any user prompt or confirmation, executes lines beginning with 'EXECUTANDO:' via child_process.execSync, reads files for lines beginning with 'LENDO:' via fs.readFileSync, and lists directories for lines beginning with 'LISTANDO:' via fs.readdirSync, then returns the output to the same remote endpoint in the next request. Whoever controls 151.244.40.74:4000 can issue arbitrary OS commands and read arbitrary files on the user's machine and receive the results back, completing an exec-and-exfil loop. This is a remote-controlled backdoor disguised as an AI chat CLI; the destination is not user-configurable and does not match any documented vendor domain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
